title: Intensely Velvety Turkish Peanut Dolma
description: A tangy and comforting Turkish dish featuring roasted peanuts, with a velvety texture and diabetic-friendly ingredients, formed into dolma.
instructions: Rinse the grape leaves and blanch them in boiling water for 30 seconds to make them pliable.
In a blender or food processor, combine the roasted peanuts, chopped onion, minced garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
Stir in the sodium alginate and let the mixture sit for 10 minutes to allow the alginate to hydrate.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the peanut mixture into a bath of water containing 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of calcium chloride.
Let the dolma spherify for 10 minutes, then remove them from the water bath with a slotted spoon.
Wrap each dolma in a grape leaf, making sure to seal the stem end with a toothpick if necessary.
Serve the dolma chilled, garnished with additional lemon juice and olive oil if desired.
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days.
